title: A Day as a Research Roboticist
setting: CSIRO's Data61 Robotics and Autonomous Systems Lab, Pullenvale, Brisbane QLD
companySize: Large government research organisation (5,000+ staff across CSIRO, ~200 in Data61 robotics)
description: Dr. Sarah Chen is a Senior Research Scientist at CSIRO's Data61, working on autonomous navigation for mining inspection robots. Her work spans algorithm development, simulation testing, and field trials at partner mine sites. She divides her time between writing code in the lab, running experiments on real robots, publishing papers, supervising PhD students, and engaging with mining industry partners to understand real-world problems. Her current project is developing SLAM algorithms that work reliably in GPS-denied underground environments with dust, low light, and dynamic obstacles like moving vehicles.
salary: $130,000 - $175,000 AUD per year (CSIRO Research Scientist level, depending on seniority and classification)

schedule.activity: Arrive at lab, review overnight simulation results
schedule.note: Batch simulations run overnight on the compute cluster, testing SLAM algorithm variants across 50 different mine environments

schedule.activity: Stand-up meeting with the autonomous systems team
schedule.note: 8-person team reviews progress, blockers, and coordinates shared resources like the robot platforms and test arena
Implementing a new loop closure detection method using scan context descriptors for 3D LiDAR data
Discussing experimental design for a paper on multi-robot collaborative SLAM in underground tunnels

schedule.activity: Hardware testing in the indoor test arena
schedule.note: Running the new SLAM algorithm on the physical robot in a mock tunnel environment built from shipping containers

schedule.activity: Lunch and informal discussion with colleagues
schedule.note: Cross-pollination with the agricultural robotics team — discussing sensor fusion techniques that might transfer across domains

schedule.activity: Write a section of the ICRA paper submission
schedule.note: Generating comparison plots and writing the experimental results section for the International Conference on Robotics and Automation

schedule.activity: Video call with mining industry partner
schedule.note: Briefing the Rio Tinto autonomous systems team on field trial results from last month's underground test at a Pilbara mine site

schedule.activity: Analyse field trial data from the mine site visit
schedule.note: Processing 3D point cloud data collected during the mine trial, comparing SLAM output against survey-grade ground truth

schedule.activity: Plan next day, update project tracker, head home
schedule.note: Tomorrow: field trial preparation — packing robot platform, spare batteries, and sensor housings for a week-long underground test

responsibilities: Develop novel algorithms for autonomous robot navigation in challenging environments
Design and conduct experiments, both in simulation and on physical robots
Publish research papers at top robotics conferences (ICRA, IROS, RSS)
Engage with industry partners to identify real-world problems and validate solutions
Maintain research software infrastructure and robot platforms
Write grant proposals and contribute to research strategy
Present work at conferences, workshops, and industry events
challenges: Bridging the gap between algorithms that work in simulation and algorithms that work in dusty, dark, vibrating mine environments
Balancing publication pressure with industry impact — academic novelty does not always align with practical value
Managing the timeline uncertainty of field trials — mine site access depends on operational schedules and safety clearances
Keeping up with the rapid pace of robotics research — hundreds of relevant papers published every month
Securing ongoing funding through competitive grant processes
rewards: Seeing your algorithms run autonomously in a real underground mine for the first time
Contributing to technology that makes mining safer by reducing human exposure to underground hazards
Intellectual freedom to explore novel ideas and define your own research direction
Working with world-class colleagues across disciplines
Presenting at international conferences and building a global professional network
The satisfaction of solving genuinely hard problems that matter to Australian industry
careerPath: Postdoctoral Researcher → Research Scientist → Senior Research Scientist → Principal Research Scientist
Alternative: transition to industry as a Robotics Engineering Lead at a mining automation company
Alternative: start a robotics spin-off company commercialising research (CSIRO's ON program supports this)
Alternative: move into a university academic position combining teaching and research
